summary.leadingMessage: Execution drag is stacking across multiple lanes and now threatens the board story more than market demand does.

items.dragTheme: Governance review load is slowing the next AI release tranche more than model capability is.
items.boardQuestion: Which flagship lane has momentum but is being slowed by preventable operating drag?
items.currentPosture: The AI control plane is real and productive, but approval cycles are starting to lengthen as more teams route requests through the same governance reviewers.
items.blockerHeadline: Approval and evaluation throughput is becoming the new release bottleneck.
items.delaySignal: Agent launches keep slipping behind governance review queue age.
items.headline: AI growth is being slowed by governance throughput, not by product scarcity.

coordinationAssessment.message: Coordination latency is now a material drag on board-backed execution.

frictionAssessment.message: Operating friction is severe enough to distort the current growth story.

lagAssessment.message: Decision lag is severe enough to undermine the current execution cadence.

deliveryAssessment.message: Delivery reliability is strong enough to absorb the current pace of change.

visibilityAssessment.message: Board visibility is clear enough to keep the operating story credible.

items.dragTheme: Identity evidence closure and privileged-review cleanup are dragging the broader admin modernization story.
items.boardQuestion: Which trust lane is slowing adjacent programs because cleanup is consuming too much operating time?
items.currentPosture: The identity estate is real, but remediation and review closure are consuming so much attention that adjacent rollout work keeps slipping.
items.blockerHeadline: Exception cleanup is starving forward progress.
items.delaySignal: Admin programs pause while reviewer backlog absorbs operating time.
items.headline: Identity drag is now a portfolio drag, not just a security queue problem.

coordinationAssessment.message: Coordination drag is rising and will slow the next board-visible milestone without tighter ownership.

frictionAssessment.message: Operating friction is compounding and should be treated as a delivery risk, not a minor process tax.

lagAssessment.message: Decision lag is stretching the time between board intent and operating action.

deliveryAssessment.message: Delivery reliability is too weak to support the current pace of expansion.

visibilityAssessment.message: Board visibility is too thin to support confident decisions.

items.dragTheme: Refresh failures and attribution disputes are slowing board-trusted growth decisions.
items.boardQuestion: Which commercial lane is creating decision drag because the underlying data is not stable enough?
items.currentPosture: Reporting surfaces are live, but board-safe growth decisions keep slowing because refresh integrity and attribution trust still need manual reconciliation.
items.blockerHeadline: Decision speed is lagging because the numbers still need explanation loops.
items.delaySignal: Growth planning meetings slip while teams reconcile reporting disagreements.
items.headline: Revenue growth is being delayed by explanation work, not just by market execution.

items.dragTheme: Fragmented diligence proofs are creating cross-team latency across payments, merchant review, treasury, and KYC.
items.boardQuestion: Which lane needs ownership reassignment because the drag is spread across too many partial control rooms?
items.currentPosture: The surfaces exist, but leadership still spends too much meeting time stitching them together before a decision is board-safe.
items.blockerHeadline: Fragmented proofs are slowing capital and risk decisions.
items.delaySignal: Merchant, treasury, and KYC reviews queue behind fragmented evidence requests.
items.headline: FinTech drag comes from fragmented ownership more than missing evidence.

items.dragTheme: Enterprise deal velocity is now limited by answer throughput and proof freshness, not by product positioning.
items.boardQuestion: Which commercial lane can move faster if trust and procurement drag is reduced?
items.currentPosture: The trust and procurement stack is strong, but answer freshness and questionnaire workload still slow the marginal enterprise deal.
items.blockerHeadline: Questionnaire throughput is the remaining drag on an otherwise strong buyer lane.
items.delaySignal: Deals stall while updated proofs and answers are assembled.
items.headline: Procurement can accelerate if answer and proof throughput is unblocked.

items.dragTheme: Regulated review continuity and staffing are creating latency across assay, CAPA, safety, and specimen lanes.
items.boardQuestion: Which regulated lane is slowing because review continuity cannot absorb the current surface count?
items.currentPosture: The vertical is real, but the number of regulated surfaces is growing faster than the review team can keep moving them forward cleanly.
items.blockerHeadline: Review continuity is the drag, not the lack of worthwhile surface area.
items.delaySignal: Release and change-control packets stack up behind the same regulated reviewers.
items.headline: Biotech drag is now a review-capacity problem the board should see directly.
